# Headroom

> Context optimization layer for LLM applications. Compress tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before they reach the model. Same answers, 60–95% fewer tokens. Python library and MCP server. Apache 2.0, local-first.

Headroom is shipped as a Python package (`headroom-ai`) with an MCP server (`headroom_compress`, `headroom_retrieve`, `headroom_stats` tools). Both use the same compression pipeline: per-content-type compressors (JSON, code, logs, diffs, text) feed into a Compress-Cache-Retrieve (CCR) store so compression stays reversible — the LLM can ask for the original whenever it wants.

## Packages

- [PyPI package](https://pypi.org/project/headroom-ai/): Python install.

## Install (copy-paste-runnable)

- Python: `pip install headroom-ai` (add `[all]` for every optional extra)
- MCP server: `pip install "headroom-ai[mcp]"` then run `python -m headroom.ccr.mcp_server`
- Compress in code: `from headroom import compress`

## Entry points

- **Quickstart**: 5-minute end-to-end (install → compress → call the model).
- **Installation**: All install paths, extras, env vars.
- **MCP server**: `headroom_compress`, `headroom_retrieve`, `headroom_stats` for Claude Code / Cursor / any MCP host.
- **API reference**: Python `compress()` API.

## How it works

- **How compression works**: Three-stage pipeline + automatic content routing.
- **SmartCrusher**: Statistical JSON / array compression (70–90% on tool outputs).
- **Code compression**: AST-aware via tree-sitter (preserves imports, signatures, types).
- **Text & log compression**: Search results, build logs, diffs.
- **CCR (reversible)**: Compress-Cache-Retrieve — originals never deleted; LLM retrieves on demand.

## Operations

- **Configuration**: Env vars, config file, per-call overrides.
- **Benchmarks**: Token-savings numbers across content types.
- **Troubleshooting**: Common failure modes and fixes.
- **Limitations**: What Headroom won't do well today.

## Licensing

Apache 2.0. Use commercially, modify, redistribute. Data stays on the user's machine when running the library or MCP server locally. Anonymous telemetry enabled by default; set `HEADROOM_TELEMETRY=off` to disable.
